description_long: This object is a rustic wooden palisade fence composed of a series of vertically aligned, sharpened wooden stakes. Each stake is roughly cylindrical and tapers to a pointed tip at the top, giving the fence a defensive, spiked appearance. The wood shows natural grain, knots, and some weathering, with a mix of light and dark brown tones, suggesting it is untreated or minimally processed. The stakes are closely packed together, forming a solid barrier with no visible gaps between them. There are no horizontal rails or decorative elements; the design is purely functional, likely intended for security or boundary marking. The overall construction is robust and utilitarian, suitable for use in parks, gardens, or as a temporary urban barrier.
description: A rustic wooden palisade fence made of closely packed, pointed wooden stakes with a natural, weathered finish. The design is simple and functional, with no decorative elements or horizontal rails.
description_view_0: A narrow side view showing the thin profile of the fence, with the pointed tops of the stakes visible.
description_view_1: A full frontal view displaying the row of pointed wooden stakes, highlighting the solid, continuous barrier they form.
description_view_2: The opposite narrow side view, similar to view 0, showing the slim edge and pointed tops.
description_view_3: A rear view nearly identical to the front, showing the same row of pointed stakes and their natural wood texture.
